issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 29/06/26

✅ Implementation: Bruno API Documentation

This document provides a highly detailed, step-by-step implementation guide to execute the exhaustive tasks outlined in API_Documentation_Task_Plan.md. It covers environment configuration, scripting, testing, and CI/CD integration based on the api-collection/Villume GO directory.

1. Initial Configuration & Environments

Before documenting individual endpoints, we must accurately map out the environments in Bruno.

1.1 Environment Setup

  1. Open Bruno and navigate to the Environments tab for the Villume GO collection.
  2. Ensure the following environments are properly configured exactly as in the environments/ folder:
    • Local - Dev
    • Dev
  3. Add a global variable token (leave the value blank initially) to store the dynamically extracted JWT/Bearer token.

1.2 Global Headers

  1. Open the Collection Settings in Bruno.
  2. Under the Headers tab, set default headers for all requests (unless overridden by specific requests):
    • Content-Type: application/json
    • Accept: application/json

1.3 Authentication Handling (Pre-request Script)

To avoid manual token entry across numerous endpoints, use Bruno's pre-request script feature at the root collection level.

  1. Open Collection Settings > Script > Pre Request.
  2. Add the following snippet:
const token = bru.getEnvVar("token");
if (token) {
  // Assuming Bearer authentication. Adjust if the API uses a different auth scheme.
  req.setHeader("Authorization", `Bearer ${token}`);
}

2. Implementing the Initial Tokens (The Foundation)

Because this project strictly requires authentication for many endpoints, you will need a valid token to test the API.

Example: Handling Authentication

You can manually inject the token in the environment variables, or use a helper endpoint from the Auth folder in the collection.

  1. Method: Obtain a token using endpoints like Direct Login or Login As User (impersonate) in the Auth directory.
  2. Post-response Script (Token Extraction): 
    const resData = res.getBody();
if (resData && resData.data && resData.data.token) {
  bru.setEnvVar("token", resData.data.token);
  console.log("Token successfully extracted and saved to environment!");
}

3. Implementing Core Features (Auth, Exhibitions, Users, Billings)

For modules containing dozens of files and subdirectories, standardization is key.

3.1 Standard Documentation Requirements

For every endpoint in these folders, the assigned developer must complete the following checklist:

  1. Path Variables: Use Bruno variables instead of hardcoded IDs.
    • Bad: GET {{base_url}}/exhibitions/detail?id=12345
    • Good: GET {{base_url}}/exhibitions/detail?id={{exhibition_id}} (and define exhibition_id in the environment or request vars).
  2. Query Parameters: Document all available filters natively in the Query tab so they are visible.
    • Example: ?limit=10&page=1
  3. Save Examples: This is crucial. Once an endpoint works against Local - Dev, click Save Example in Bruno. This caches the response payload, allowing front-end devs to mock the API without running the backend.

3.2 Testing Endpoints

Many endpoints depend heavily on payload data or query string parameters.

  1. Map out all required parameters.
  2. Include at least two saved examples: one for a successful request (200 OK) and one for an expected failure or empty state.

4. Background Tasks (Dev, Logs, Support)

These endpoints often have different authentication requirements or bypass standard user auth.

  1. Dev Endpoints: Check endpoints like Worker Email and Generate Token for specific server-to-server requirements and document them in the Notes tab.
  2. Logs: Endpoints related to analytics like Total Visitor Unique should have clear examples of the response structure.

5. Export and CLI Integration (CI/CD)

Once the documentation is robust and tests are written, integrate it into your automated pipelines.

  1. Install Bruno CLI globally: 
    npm install -g @usebruno/cli
  2. Run the collection locally (for testing): 
    bru run --env "Local - Dev"
  3. Generate Static HTML Documentation: 
    bru export --format html --output api-docs.html
  4. CI/CD Pipeline (GitHub Actions Example):
    Create a workflow file .github/workflows/bruno.yml: 
    name: Run API Tests
on: [push]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      - run: npm install -g @usebruno/cli
      - run: bru run --env Dev

This document is the exhaustive checklist for building, organizing, and maintaining the API documentation using Bruno, based on the exact contents of the api-collection/Villume GO directory.

Source: docs/task/API_Documentation_Task_Plan.md

Phase 1: Setup and Environment Configuration

  • Initialize Bruno Collection: Verify and refine the opencollection.yml configuration.
  • Configure Environments: Map variables for Local - Dev and Dev inside environments/.
  • Global Setup: Configure global headers (e.g., Content-Type, Accept).
  • Authentication Handling: Set up pre-request scripts / collection-level auth to inject Bearer tokens for protected routes.
  • Root Endpoints: Check Health.

Phase 2: Additional Features

  • Root Endpoints: Get Latest Version.

Phase 3: Auth Features

  • Root Endpoints: Check OTP, Check Password, Check username, Direct Login, Forgot Password, Google, Login As User (impersonate), Manual, Register Manual, Resend OTP, Reset Password, Update Password, Validate Recaptcha.

Phase 4: Billings Features

  • Sub-modules: Card, Invoices, products.
  • Card: Add Card, Delete Card, Detail card default, Edit Card, List Cards.
  • Invoices: Create Invoice, Download, Histories.
  • products: Detail Pricing User, List Billing Product, Product Detail.
  • Root Endpoints: Create Checkout Session, Create Customer, Create Subcription, Subcribe, Success Checkout, Unsubscribe, Update Customer, User Info.

Phase 5: Dev Features

  • Root Endpoints: Generate Token, Worker Email.

Phase 6: Exhibitions Features

  • Sub-modules: Cover Image, Frame, Splash Screen, Upload.
  • Cover Image: Delete Cover Image, Upload Cover Image.
  • Frame: Add Frame Template, Delete Frame Template, Edit Frame Template, List Frame Template, Upload Frame Texture.
  • Splash Screen: Get Splash Screen, Update Splash Screen.
  • Upload: Add Text Wall, Replace Dimention, Replace File, Save Changes Text Wall, Upload Figure, Upload Object on Artwork, Upload Ordinary Object, Upload Video.
    • Audio Video: Delete Audio Support, Delete Video Support, Upload Audio Support, Upload Video Support.
  • Root Endpoints: Check Viewer Json, Create Exhibition, Delete Exhibition, Get Audio, Get Exhibition Detail, Get Model Path Size, Get Video, Process Create Exhibition, Process Publish Exhibition, Publish Exhibition, Save Changes Camera, Save Changes Figure, Save Changes Objects, Save Changes Text Wall, Save Changes Viewer JSON, Save Changes, Save Config, UnPublish Exhibition, Update Viewer Json, Validation Create Exhibition, Validation Publish Exhibition.

Phase 7: Logs Features

  • Root Endpoints: Add, Artwork Interaction, Average Interaction Artwork, Average Request Artwork, Details Visitors IP, Graphic Visitors per day, List Visitor Unique, List Visitors, Top Browser, Top Countries, Top Device, Total Artwork Interaction, Total Visitor Unique, Total Visitor.

Phase 8: Room Features

  • Root Endpoints: Get List Room.

Phase 9: Support Features

  • Sub-modules: Coupon.
  • Coupon: Check, Create, Delete Coupon, Detail Coupon, Edit, List Coupon.
  • Root Endpoints: Contact Us, List Emails, List Product For Support, List Users.

Phase 10: Users Features

  • Root Endpoints: Deactive User, Detail, Edit Profile, Get User by Username, Is This Me -, Update Account Setting, Update Favorite Colors, Update Json Version, Update User Info, Upload Avatar, User Is Back.

Phase 11: Viewers Features

  • Root Endpoints: Get Exhibition by Username, Get Other Published Exhibition User, Get User Profile by Share String, Get Viewer Json, Validation Get Viewer.

Phase 12: White Label Features

  • Root Endpoints: Gallery, Profile.

Phase 99: Enhancement and Automation

  • Add Request/Response Examples: Ensure every request in Bruno has at least one saved example response.
  • Write Tests (Assertions): Write basic Bruno test scripts to assert status codes (e.g., expect(res.getStatus()).to.equal(200)).
  • CI/CD Integration (Optional): Set up the Bruno CLI (bru) in the CI pipeline to run the collection as an automated test suite.
  • Static Site Generation: Generate static HTML API documentation using the Bruno CLI.
